nativeRange.habitat: Reef slopes, overhangs, and sheltered areas on tropical reefs, from well-lit upper zones to more dimly lit mid-depth environments

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae symbionts; lacks visible polyps and tentacles. Has been reported to benefit from supplemental phytoplankton additions a few times per week. Calcium, strontium, magnesium, and trace element supplementation are generally advised to support skeletal growth.

avoidWith: aggressive LPS corals with long sweeper tentacles placed in direct contact

summary: Pachyseris is a plating/cup SPS coral (family Agariciidae) prized for its distinctive ridged, elephant-skin surface texture. Intermediate care; benefits from moderate light and flow with stable reef water chemistry.
careNotes:
Note: Pachyseris is a genus with multiple species (including P. speciosa, P. rugosa, P. inattesa) sold collectively in the aquarium trade under shared common names. The scientificName field is set to 'Pachyseris sp.' to reflect this genus-level trade convention, consistent with how LiveAquaria lists the coral.

Pachyseris corals are members of the family Agariciidae and are sometimes categorized alongside SPS corals in the aquarium trade, though they are technically more tolerant of lower light than classic SPS genera like Acropora or Montipora. They form plating or cup-shaped colonies with characteristic parallel ridges and valleys that give rise to the common names 'Elephant Skin Coral' and 'Corduroy Coral.'

Key care notes:
• Lighting: Generally advised to keep at moderate PAR levels (around 100–250 PAR). Unlike many SPS corals, Pachyseris has been found on reef slopes and under overhangs in the wild, suggesting some adaptability to lower-light conditions. New specimens are best acclimated gradually, starting at lower light and moving up if desired.
• Flow: Moderate water movement is generally recommended. Strong direct laminar flow may stress the tissue; alternating or randomized moderate flow tends to work well.
• Placement: Middle of the rockwork is typically advised, though this coral can tolerate a range of positions.
• Aggression: Pachyseris lacks visible polyps or sweeper tentacles, making it among the most peaceful of SPS corals. It is generally considered reef-safe with respect to neighbors, though standard coral-spacing practices are still advisable.
• Supplements: Calcium, alkalinity (kH), magnesium, strontium, and trace elements are generally advised to support skeletal calcification, consistent with standard SPS reef chemistry.
• Growth form: Colonies develop as encrusting plates that follow the underlying substrate contour, which can make them appear slow-growing but are steady growers under good conditions.

Cross-source verification note: Only LiveAquaria successfully yielded a Pachyseris-specific husbandry page during research (accessed 2026-06-04). All other attempted secondaries (Reef2Reef wiki, Bulk Reef Supply, AquaticCommunity, ReefBuilders) either returned 404, 403, or did not carry a Pachyseris-specific care guide. Wild ecology data (depth range 10–35 m, colony diameter 10–25 cm) was supplemented from a ReefBuilders article on P. inattesa (a Red Sea congener). The single-source confirmation triggers needs_review status per the playbook.
breedingNotes: Fragging is the primary propagation method in captivity. Plating colonies can be fragmented with bone cutters or a bandsaw; fragments readily encrust onto frag plugs or rubble pieces. Sexual reproduction occurs via broadcast spawning on natural reefs but is not practiced in the aquarium hobby.

primary.notes: Primary source. Provided care level (Moderate), common names (Elephant Skin, Corduroy, Castle Coral), temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), specific gravity (1.023–1.025), dKH (8–12), temperament (Peaceful), lighting description, flow description, and feeding notes.

Not used for husbandry parameters. This source does not confirm high-stakes fields and does not count toward the minimum-confirmation bar.
